texto: Be sure not to turn your life into a series of tasks which must always be performed... I know it can seem appealing to live in a state of accomplishing things, always succeeding, always getting things done. But consider this. A whole life lost. A sacrifice to your fragile sense of order.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Kevin Hooyman sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “Be sure not to turn your life into a series of tasks which must always be performed... I know it …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a vida, life-philosophy, order, útil em momentos de trabalho e order.

texto: Hey man let me ask you something. Do you sort of feel like you are carrying a low level anxiety about the existence of shark attacks? I mean, just the fact that it really happens, it's horrible, it's horrible enough that you kind of have to worry about it, at least a little bit, almost all the time...
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Kevin Hooyman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Hey man let me ask you something. Do you sort of feel like you are carrying a low level anxiety a…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, anxiety, about pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

texto: Do you think that the people on reality T.V. shows are kind of good actors? I mean they would have to be! You can't ignore all the cameras and, you know, the whole situation of being on a reality show... But here is what I'm wondering. Do you think that they think of themselves as actors? Or do you think that they are living their lives behind an impenetrable shield? Like their entire approach to life is the same as an actor's approach to a performance? So when they had to act natural in an unnatural situation it was sort of no big deal.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Kevin Hooyman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “Do you think that the people on reality T.V. shows are kind of good actors? I mean they would hav…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, acting, alienation pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de alienation e life-philosophy.

frase_original: Do you think that the people on reality T.V. shows are kind of good actors? I mean they would have to be! You can't ignore all the cameras and, you know, the whole situation of being on a reality show... But here is what I'm wondering. Do you think that they think of themselves as actors? Or do you think that they are living their lives behind an impenetrable shield? Like their entire approach to life is the same as an actor's approach to a performance? So when they had to act natural in an unnatural situation it was sort of no big deal.
